content:
Let’s place the rectangle so that 
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), D=(0,12), C=(5,12). 
Since dist (E,AB)=12 and dist (E,AD)=1 and ∆ABE is acute, one checks that the only possibility is  
E=(1,−12).  

Any line through E meeting AB at F=(t,0) meets the top side y=12 at  
G=E+2(F−E)=(1,−12)+2((t,0)−(1,−12))=(2t−1, 12).  
To bisect the area of the 5×12 rectangle we want the quadrilateral B–C–G–F to have area 30.  By the shoelace formula its area is  
½|(5·12+5·12+(2t−1)·0+t·0)−(0·5+12·(2t−1)+12·t+0·5)|  
=½|120−(36t−12)|=66−18t.  
Setting 66−18t=30 ⇒ t=2.  Hence  
F=(2,0),  G=(2·2−1,12)=(3,12)  
so  
FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.  

Answer:  \boxed{145}.

message.content:

To bisect the area of the 5×12 rectangle we want the quadrilateral B–C–G–F to have area 30.  By the shoelace formula its area is  
½|(5·12+5·12+(2t−1)·0+t·0)−(0·5+12·(2t−1)+12·t+0·5)|

Setting 66−18t=30 ⇒ t=2.  Hence

Answer:  \boxed{145}.
